question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getSenderAccountID()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a request with a non-zero sender address and non-zero value where the account exists but has a key equal to IMMUTABILITY_SENTINEL_KEY, causing getSenderAccountID() to write to writableAccountCache with DEFAULT_KEY, and by flooding with such requests cause unbounded write cache growth that exhausts node memory?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: execute()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a timeout on executor.execute(transactionBody, Instant.now(), getOperationTracers()) to submit transactions with computationally expensive EVM bytecode that runs indefinitely, tying up node threads and preventing other requests from being processed?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getOperationTracers()] Can an unprivileged external user manipulate the ContractCallContext to have a non-null opcodeContext, causing getOperationTracers() to return opcodeActionTracer instead of mirrorOperationActionTracer, and exploit differences in tracer behavior to trigger an unhandled exception or resource exhaustion in the opcode tracer?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: handleFailedResult()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a failed transaction where ContractCallContext.get().getOpcodeContext() is non-null, causing handleFailedResult() to return a failed EvmTransactionResult instead of throwing, and exploit this silent failure path to bypass error handling and cause downstream state corruption that degrades node stability?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: buildEthereumTransactionBody()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a ContractDebugParameters with ethereumData of exactly 1 byte (length > 0 check passes), causing Bytes.wrap() to succeed but populateEthTxData() to fail with an unchecked parsing exception during patchSenderNonce(), crashing the node's request handler?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: execute()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the synchronized create() method in TransactionExecutorFactory to cause a thread contention deadlock by flooding with requests that trigger transactionExecutors.computeIfAbsent(version, this::create), starving other threads and causing node-wide request processing to halt?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: buildContractCreateTransactionBody()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a contract-create request with a Long.MAX_VALUE estimatedGas, causing the ContractCreateTransactionBody to be built with an extremely large gas value that triggers integer overflow or unexpected behavior in the executor, crashing the node?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: populateChildTransactionErrors()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction that generates thousands of child transaction records all with non-SUCCESS/non-REVERTED_SUCCESS statuses, causing populateChildTransactionErrors() to build a massive LinkedHashSet of error strings, exhausting heap memory and crashing the node?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getSenderAccountIDAsNum()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a request with a null senderAddress, causing ConversionUtils.isLongZero(senderAddress) to throw a NullPointerException inside getSenderAccountIDAsNum(), propagating up through getSenderAccountID() and execute() as an unhandled exception that crashes the node?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: convertAddressToProtoBytes()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a request with an Address whose toArrayUnsafe() returns a null or zero-length array, causing Bytes.wrap() in convertAddressToProtoBytes() to produce an invalid ProtoBytes that corrupts the alias lookup and triggers an unhandled exception in aliasesReadableKVState.get()?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: execute()] Can an unprivileged external user submit concurrent contract-create and contract-call requests that both write to the same ContractCallContext write cache simultaneously, causing a race condition in the HashMap-backed writeCache that corrupts shared state and crashes multiple node threads?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: buildSuccessResult()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction that returns SUCCESS with an empty transactionRecords list, causing transactionRecords.getFirst() to throw a NoSuchElementException inside buildSuccessResult(), propagating as an unhandled exception that crashes the node's request handler?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: handleFailedResult()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a failed transaction where the parentTransactionRecord has a null receipt, causing parentTransactionRecord.receiptOrThrow() to throw an unchecked exception inside handleFailedResult(), propagating up and crashing the node?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: patchSenderNonce()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a ContractDebugParameters where ContractCallContext.isInitialized() returns true but ContractCallContext.get() returns a context with a null writeCache, causing writableCache.put() to throw a NullPointerException that crashes the node?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: execute()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that Instant.now() is called inside executor.execute() to submit transactions with a manipulated system clock (via NTP poisoning or similar), causing transaction validity checks to fail in bulk and flooding nodes with error-handling overhead that degrades availability?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: buildContractCallTransactionBody()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a contract-call request with a receiver address whose toArrayUnsafe() returns a 32-byte array instead of 20 bytes, causing Bytes.wrap() to produce an oversized evmAddress in ContractID that triggers an unexpected exception in the executor?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getSenderAccountID()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a request with a sender address that resolves to a smart contract account (account.smartContract() == true), causing getSenderAccountID() to silently substitute the treasury account as sender, and exploit this substitution to bypass sender-based rate limiting and flood nodes with treasury-attributed requests?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: execute()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a large number of concurrent requests that each call evmProperties.getVersionedConfiguration() and configuration.getConfigData(EntitiesConfig.class).maxLifetime(), causing lock contention on the configuration object and starving other threads, degrading node throughput to the point of effective shutdown?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: buildEthereumTransactionBody()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a ContractDebugParameters with ethereumData containing a crafted RLP structure that causes populateEthTxData() to enter an infinite loop during parsing, tying up the node thread indefinitely and preventing other requests from being processed?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: populateChildTransactionErrors()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction that produces child records with extremely long status protoName() strings, causing childTransactionErrors.add(status.protoName()) to accumulate a large LinkedHashSet of long strings, exhausting heap memory and crashing the node?
